steps.title: Find the defining clue
steps.content: The author explains what “I couldn't be stopped” means through the evidence: “I was loud and unrelenting.” The word unrelenting is the decisive signal—it means relentless and nonstop, directly explaining why she couldn't be stopped from talking.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D says she “seemed to talk all the time,” which perfectly captures the relentless talking implied by “unrelenting” and “loud.” The other three choices address unrelated behaviors—sitting still, changing her mind, and being liked—none of which connect to her sister's friends barely tolerating her or her persistent speech.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
